decisions.title_en: Isomorphic Labs closes $2.1B Series B led by Thrive Capital
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. May 12 announcement: $2.1B Series B led by Thrive Capital (2nd consecutive lead), with Alphabet/GV/MGX/Temasek/CapitalG/UK Sovereign AI Fund. Funding scales IsoDDE drug design engine (claimed >2x AlphaFold 3 accuracy).

decisions.title_en: Sam Altman takes the stand in Musk OpenAI trial Day 7
decisions.reason: Previously published 5-5 (Brockman testimony / Musk $800B Mars plan) and 5-7 (Zilis Tesla-board scoop) and 5-12 (Satya Nadella testimony). Today is substantively new: Altman himself takes the stand for the first time, testifies that Musk wanted 90% equity, recounts 'hair-raising moment' over Tesla portrait, and discusses 2023 board ouster context.

decisions.title_en: OpenAI Daybreak cyber platform launches with Cisco/Oracle/CrowdStrike
decisions.reason: Previously published 5-7 (GPT-5.5-Cyber preview launch) and 5-12 (EU rollout). New on 5-11: OpenAI launches productized 'Daybreak' platform built on GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.5-Cyber + new 'Codex Security' agent + 8 launch partners (Cisco/Oracle/CrowdStrike/Palo Alto/Cloudflare/Fortinet/Akamai/Zscaler). This is a substantive product launch beyond the underlying model itself.

decisions.title_en: Anthropic launches Claude for Legal with Thomson Reuters CoCounsel + Harvey
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. May 12 announcement: 12 practice-area plugins (commercial, employment, litigation, law student, etc.) + 20+ connectors (Box, Everlaw, DocuSign), Thomson Reuters CoCounsel MCP integration (1.9B Westlaw docs), Harvey connector, Free Law Project / Justice Technology Association access partnerships.

decisions.title_en: Google unveils Googlebook AI-native laptop killing Chromebook brand
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. May 12 Android Show I/O Edition reveal: Googlebook replaces Chromebook, designed entirely around Gemini Intelligence; Magic Pointer summons Gemini via cursor wiggle; multiple OEM partners (Acer/Asus/Dell/HP/Lenovo); 'glowbar' design language; ships later 2026.

decisions.title_en: Mira Murati Thinking Machines unveils Interaction Models, 0.4s full-duplex
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. May 11 research preview: TML-Interaction-Small responds in <0.4s on FD-bench, simultaneously processes audio/video/text in 200ms micro-turns. Mira Murati's $12B Thinking Machines Lab first public model release, claiming every major lab built interaction layer 'wrong' as architectural afterthought.

decisions.title_en: Japan PM Takaichi orders cabinet-level Mythos cyber defense review
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. 5-5 amodei-mythos-moment-of-danger covered Amodei's '6-12 month danger window'; this is a distinct story — first major foreign government cabinet-level response. PM Takaichi instructs cyber minister Hisashi Matsumoto to audit government / critical infrastructure for Mythos-style exploitation. Finance Minister Katayama coordinated with US Treasury's Bessent in Tokyo same day.

decisions.title_en: GM cuts 600 IT staff (10%) in AI-engineer rehiring swap
decisions.reason: story_key not seen in lookback. May 12 disclosure: GM cuts ~600 IT roles (10% of IT workforce) while simultaneously hiring AI-native engineers, data engineers, prompt specialists. First major auto-OEM to publicly execute a 'cut IT, hire AI' selective swap.

decisions.title_en: Cerebras Nasdaq IPO opens May 14 — pre-market roadshow trading
decisions.reason: Already published 5-12 (Cerebras IPO upsize to $48.8B, +30% pricing). IPO is May 14; no substantive new development today between yesterday's filing and tomorrow's open.

decisions.title_en: Trump delegation departs for Beijing May 13
decisions.reason: Already published 5-12 (full delegation list incl. Musk/Cook/Fink, Huang excluded). Trump arrives in Beijing today but the substantive summit content (AI chip talks, AI safety dialogue) only emerges 5-14/5-15. Same-day arrival without new readouts is not substantive new information.
